articles.headline: Why England vs. Argentina is World Cup's best, angriest, most chaotic rivalry
articles.description: Wednesday will see England and Argentina meet for the first time since 2005, and it's a rivalry that has defined the World Cup thanks to some unforgettable encounters. Here's why this game is so compelling.

articles.headline: What is the Hand of God? Why did Diego Maradona's goal in 1986 stand?
articles.description: There are few goals in football history that have generated as much debate, legacy and, in many corners, furor as Diego Maradona's "Hand of God" -- scored at the 1986 World Cup against England.

articles.headline: Harry Kane: England can't be dragged into emotional baggage of Argentina game

article.headline: Lionel Messi bags two WC records as Argentina beat Austria
article.description: Lionel Messi set a World Cup record with his 17th and 18th goals, and defending champion Argentina advanced to the knockout stage with a 2-0 victory over Austria on Monday.

images.name: Lionel Messi makes World Cup history in win over Austria
images.caption: Lionel Messi scores a historic brace and his 18th WC goal to become the World Cup's all-time leading goal scorer in Argentina's 2-0 victory against Austria.

article.story:
ARLINGTON, Texas -- Lionel Messi set a FIFA World Cup record with his 17th and 18th goals, and defending champion Argentina advanced to the knockout stage with a 2-0 victory over Austria on Monday.

Messi had a golden opportunity to break the record in the ninth minute, but went wide to the right on a penalty kick. Almost 30 minutes later, Messi caught Alexander Schlager leaning the wrong way after Thiago Almada let Facuno Medina's pass go by him directly onto Messi's left foot.

Messi scored again in injury time to move two ahead of Germany striker Miroslav Klose. The Argentina captain also moved ahead of Klose for all-time wins at a World Cup, with Messi now at 18 individual victories on world soccer's biggest stage.

The win for Argentina keeps them atop Group J and ensures they will be in the knockout rounds of the World Cup. They finish group play on Saturday against Jordan.

Information from The Associated Press was used in this recap.
